question: 1. What is the maximum of \( \left|z^{3}-z+2\right| \); where \( z \) is a complex number with \( |z|=1 ? \)
solution:
Solution. Let \( f(z)=z^{3}-z+2 \). We may as well maximize \( |f(z)|^{2} \). If \( |z|=1 \), then \( z=x+i y \), where \( y^{2}=1-x^{2} \) and \( -1 \leq x \leq 1 \), so
\[
\begin{aligned}
|f(z)|^{2} & =\left|(x+i y)^{3}-(x+i y)+2\right|^{2} \\
& =\left|x^{3}-3 x\left(1-x^{2}\right)-x+2+i y\left(3 x^{2}-\left(1-x^{2}\right)-1\right)\right|^{2} \\
& =\left(4 x^{3}-4 x+2\right)^{2}+\left(1-x^{2}\right)\left(4 x^{2}-2\right)^{2} \\
& =16 x^{3}-4 x^{2}-16 x+8=L(x) .
\end{aligned}
\]

Hence we seek \( \max _{-1 \leq x \leq 1} L(x) \). This maximum must be attained either at a critical point or at an endpoint. The critical points, obtained by solving \( L^{\prime}(x)=48 x^{2}-8 x-16=0 \), are \( x=-\frac{1}{2}, \frac{2}{3} \). Since
\[
L(-1)=4, \quad L\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=13, \quad L\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)=\frac{8}{27}, \quad L(1)=4,
\]
the maximum value of \( L \) is 13 , attained for \( x=-\frac{1}{2} \). Hence the maximum value of \( |f(z)| \) on the unit circle is \( \sqrt{13} \), attained when \( \operatorname{Re} z=-\frac{1}{2} \), i.e., when \( z=(-1 \pm i \sqrt{3}) / 2 \).

descriptive_long.question: 1. What is the maximum of \( \left|complexz^{3}-complexz+2\right| \); where \( complexz \) is a complex number with \( |complexz|=1 ? \)
descriptive_long.solution:
Solution. Let \( cubicfn(complexz)=complexz^{3}-complexz+2 \). We may as well maximize \( |cubicfn(complexz)|^{2} \). If \( |complexz|=1 \), then \( complexz=realxval+i\,realyval \), where \( realyval^{2}=1-realxval^{2} \) and \( -1 \leq realxval \leq 1 \), so
\[
\begin{aligned}
|cubicfn(complexz)|^{2} & =\left|(realxval+i\,realyval)^{3}-(realxval+i\,realyval)+2\right|^{2} \\
& =\left|realxval^{3}-3\,realxval\left(1-realxval^{2}\right)-realxval+2+i\,realyval\left(3\,realxval^{2}-\left(1-realxval^{2}\right)-1\right)\right|^{2} \\
& =\left(4\,realxval^{3}-4\,realxval+2\right)^{2}+\left(1-realxval^{2}\right)\left(4\,realxval^{2}-2\right)^{2} \\
& =16\,realxval^{3}-4\,realxval^{2}-16\,realxval+8=helperl(realxval) .
\end{aligned}
\]

Hence we seek \( \max_{-1 \leq realxval \leq 1} helperl(realxval) \). This maximum must be attained either at a critical point or at an endpoint. The critical points, obtained by solving \( helperl^{\prime}(realxval)=48\,realxval^{2}-8\,realxval-16=0 \), are \( realxval=-\frac{1}{2},\,\frac{2}{3} \). Since
\[
helperl(-1)=4, \quad helperl\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=13, \quad helperl\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)=\frac{8}{27}, \quad helperl(1)=4,
\]
the maximum value of \( helperl \) is 13, attained for \( realxval=-\frac{1}{2} \). Hence the maximum value of \( |cubicfn(complexz)| \) on the unit circle is \( \sqrt{13} \), attained when \( \operatorname{Re} complexz=-\frac{1}{2} \), i.e., when \( complexz=(-1 \pm i \sqrt{3})/2 \).

descriptive_long_confusing.question: 1. What is the maximum of \( \left|sandstone^{3}-sandstone+2\right| \); where \( sandstone \) is a complex number with \( |sandstone|=1 ? \)
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
Solution. Let \( cardboard(sandstone)=sandstone^{3}-sandstone+2 \). We may as well maximize \( |cardboard(sandstone)|^{2} \). If \( |sandstone|=1 \), then \( sandstone=telescope+i bootlace \), where \( bootlace^{2}=1-telescope^{2} \) and \( -1 \leq telescope \leq 1 \), so
\[
\begin{aligned}
|cardboard(sandstone)|^{2} & =\left|(telescope+i bootlace)^{3}-(telescope+i bootlace)+2\right|^{2} \\
& =\left|telescope^{3}-3 telescope\left(1-telescope^{2}\right)-telescope+2+i bootlace\left(3 telescope^{2}-\left(1-telescope^{2}\right)-1\right)\right|^{2} \\
& =\left(4 telescope^{3}-4 telescope+2\right)^{2}+\left(1-telescope^{2}\right)\left(4 telescope^{2}-2\right)^{2} \\
& =16 telescope^{3}-4 telescope^{2}-16 telescope+8=sunflower(telescope) .
\end{aligned}
\]

Hence we seek \( \max _{-1 \leq telescope \leq 1} sunflower(telescope) \). This maximum must be attained either at a critical point or at an endpoint. The critical points, obtained by solving \( sunflower^{\prime}(telescope)=48 telescope^{2}-8 telescope-16=0 \), are \( telescope=-\frac{1}{2}, \frac{2}{3} \). Since
\[
sunflower(-1)=4, \quad sunflower\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=13, \quad sunflower\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)=\frac{8}{27}, \quad sunflower(1)=4,
\]
the maximum value of \( sunflower \) is 13 , attained for \( telescope=-\frac{1}{2} \). Hence the maximum value of \(|cardboard(sandstone)|\) on the unit circle is \( \sqrt{13} \), attained when \( \operatorname{Re} sandstone=-\frac{1}{2} \), i.e., when \( sandstone=(-1 \pm i \sqrt{3}) / 2 \).

descriptive_long_misleading.question: 1. What is the maximum of \( \left|realconstant^{3}-realconstant+2\right| \); where \( realconstant \) is a complex number with \( |realconstant|=1 ? \)
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
Solution. Let \( staticvalue(realconstant)=realconstant^{3}-realconstant+2 \). We may as well maximize \( |staticvalue(realconstant)|^{2} \). If \( |realconstant|=1 \), then \( realconstant=imaginaryaxis+i realcoordinate \), where \( realcoordinate^{2}=1-imaginaryaxis^{2} \) and \( -1 \leq imaginaryaxis \leq 1 \), so
\[
\begin{aligned}
|staticvalue(realconstant)|^{2} & =\left|(imaginaryaxis+i realcoordinate)^{3}-(imaginaryaxis+i realcoordinate)+2\right|^{2} \
& =\left|imaginaryaxis^{3}-3 imaginaryaxis\left(1-imaginaryaxis^{2}\right)-imaginaryaxis+2+i realcoordinate\left(3 imaginaryaxis^{2}-\left(1-imaginaryaxis^{2}\right)-1\right)\right|^{2} \
& =\left(4 imaginaryaxis^{3}-4 imaginaryaxis+2\right)^{2}+\left(1-imaginaryaxis^{2}\right)\left(4 imaginaryaxis^{2}-2\right)^{2} \
& =16 imaginaryaxis^{3}-4 imaginaryaxis^{2}-16 imaginaryaxis+8=variablestate(imaginaryaxis) .
\end{aligned}
\]

Hence we seek \( \max _{-1 \leq imaginaryaxis \leq 1} variablestate(imaginaryaxis) \). This maximum must be attained either at a critical point or at an endpoint. The critical points, obtained by solving \( variablestate^{\prime}(imaginaryaxis)=48 imaginaryaxis^{2}-8 imaginaryaxis-16=0 \), are \( imaginaryaxis=-\frac{1}{2}, \frac{2}{3} \). Since
\[
variablestate(-1)=4, \quad variablestate\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=13, \quad variablestate\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)=\frac{8}{27}, \quad variablestate(1)=4,
\]
the maximum value of \( variablestate \) is 13 , attained for \( imaginaryaxis=-\frac{1}{2} \). Hence the maximum value of \( |staticvalue(realconstant)| \) on the unit circle is \( \sqrt{13} \), attained when \( \operatorname{Re} realconstant=-\frac{1}{2} \), i.e., when \( realconstant=(-1 \pm i \sqrt{3}) / 2 \).

garbled_string.question: 1. What is the maximum of \( \left|qzxwvtnp^{3}-qzxwvtnp+2\right| \); where \( qzxwvtnp \) is a complex number with \( |qzxwvtnp|=1 ? \)
garbled_string.solution:
Solution. Let \( sbnvkeqr(qzxwvtnp)=qzxwvtnp^{3}-qzxwvtnp+2 \). We may as well maximize \( |sbnvkeqr(qzxwvtnp)|^{2} \). If \( |qzxwvtnp|=1 \), then \( qzxwvtnp=hjgrksla+i pmctgour \), where \( pmctgour^{2}=1-hjgrksla^{2} \) and \( -1 \leq hjgrksla \leq 1 \), so
\[
\begin{aligned}
|sbnvkeqr(qzxwvtnp)|^{2} & =\left|(hjgrksla+i pmctgour)^{3}-(hjgrksla+i pmctgour)+2\right|^{2} \\
& =\left|hjgrksla^{3}-3 hjgrksla\left(1-hjgrksla^{2}\right)-hjgrksla+2+i pmctgour\left(3 hjgrksla^{2}-\left(1-hjgrksla^{2}\right)-1\right)\right|^{2} \\
& =\left(4 hjgrksla^{3}-4 hjgrksla+2\right)^{2}+\left(1-hjgrksla^{2}\right)\left(4 hjgrksla^{2}-2\right)^{2} \\
& =16 hjgrksla^{3}-4 hjgrksla^{2}-16 hjgrksla+8=xfumthco(hjgrksla) .
\end{aligned}
\]

Hence we seek \( \max _{-1 \leq hjgrksla \leq 1} xfumthco(hjgrksla) \). This maximum must be attained either at a critical point or at an endpoint. The critical points, obtained by solving \( xfumthco^{\prime}(hjgrksla)=48 hjgrksla^{2}-8 hjgrksla-16=0 \), are \( hjgrksla=-\frac{1}{2}, \frac{2}{3} \). Since
\[
xfumthco(-1)=4, \quad xfumthco\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=13, \quad xfumthco\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)=\frac{8}{27}, \quad xfumthco(1)=4,
\]
 the maximum value of \( xfumthco \) is 13 , attained for \( hjgrksla=-\frac{1}{2} \). Hence the maximum value of \( |sbnvkeqr(qzxwvtnp)| \) on the unit circle is \( \sqrt{13} \), attained when \( \operatorname{Re} qzxwvtnp=-\frac{1}{2} \), i.e., when \( qzxwvtnp=(-1 \pm i \sqrt{3}) / 2 \).

kernel_variant.question:
Let  
  f(z)=z^6-4z^3+48.  
Determine the maximum value of |f(z)| when the complex number z lies on the circle |z|=2, and list every point on that circle at which the maximum is attained.
kernel_variant.solution:
Write z in polar form z=2e^{i\theta } (-\pi \leq \theta <\pi ).  Then  
  z^3=8e^{3i\theta }, z^6=64e^{6i\theta },  
so  
  f(z)=64e^{6i\theta }-32e^{3i\theta }+48.                                     (1)

Introduce t=e^{3i\theta }; then |t|=1 and e^{6i\theta }=t^2.  From (1)  
  f(z)=16(4t^2-2t+3).                                                 (2)

Put t=x+iy with x^2+y^2=1.  From (2)  

 Re f=16[4(x^2-y^2)-2x+3]=16(8x^2-2x-1),  
 Im f=16\cdot y(8x-2).

Hence  
 |f(z)|^2=16^2[(8x^2-2x-1)^2+(8x-2)^2(1-x^2)].                           (3)

A direct expansion of the bracket in (3) gives  

 (8x^2-2x-1)^2+(8x-2)^2(1-x^2)=48x^2-28x+5.                             (4)

Since 48x^2-28x+5 is an upward-opening quadratic on [-1,1], its maximum occurs at an end-point.  
 For x=1: 48-28+5=25.  
 For x=-1: 48+28+5=81 (larger).

Substituting the larger value into (3):  
 |f(z)|_{\max}=16\cdot \sqrt{81}=16\cdot 9=144.                                     (5)

The maximum arises when x=Re t=-1, i.e. t=-1=e^{i\pi }.  Because t=e^{3i\theta }, we need 3\theta \equiv \pi  (mod 2\pi ); hence \theta =\pi /3, \pi , 5\pi /3.  Consequently  

 z=2e^{i\pi /3}=1+i\sqrt{3}, z=-2, z=2e^{i5\pi /3}=1-i\sqrt{3.}                    (6)

Therefore |f(z)| attains its maximum value 144 exactly at the three points z=-2, 1+i\sqrt{3}, and 1-i\sqrt{3} on the circle |z|=2.

_replacement_note.reason: Original kernel variant was too easy compared to the original problem
